name: Weight Loss / Obesity
avoidIf: personal/family history of medullary thyroid carcinoma or MEN 2 (GLP-1/GIP agents)
notes: GLP-1 (semaglutide) or dual GIP/GLP-1 (tirzepatide) first-line; tirzepatide tends to yield greater loss. Add metformin for insulin resistance, or naltrexone/bupropion if cravings/mood predominate.

name: Type 2 Diabetes / Insulin Resistance / Metabolic Syndrome
notes: Metformin first-line for insulin sensitivity (also longevity rationale). Add GLP-1/GIP agents if weight and glycemia both need addressing. Berberine is a botanical alternative — screen CYP3A4 interactions.

name: Low Testosterone / Male Hypogonadism
avoidIf: active desire for fertility (use hCG-sparing approach)
workup: two AM total testosterone + free testosterone + SHBG
notes: Confirm biochemical hypogonadism on two morning levels before treating. Add hCG to preserve fertility/testicular volume. Reserve anastrozole only for confirmed symptomatic high estradiol — avoid routine use.

name: Menopause / Perimenopause (Women)
notes: Transdermal estradiol preferred (lower VTE/stroke risk). ALWAYS pair with progesterone if uterus is intact. Initiate within 10 years of menopause / before age 60 for best benefit-risk. Low-dose testosterone for persistent low libido.

name: Low Female Libido / HSDD
workup: rule out reversible causes (medications, mood, relationship)
notes: PT-141 (FDA-approved Vyleesi) for premenopausal HSDD. Low-dose transdermal testosterone supports desire (ISSWSH). Oxytocin and DHEA as adjuncts.

name: Erectile Dysfunction (Men)
notes: PDE5 inhibitors first-line — daily low-dose tadalafil for spontaneity, on-demand sildenafil otherwise. PT-141 (central) for PDE5 non-responders. Always exclude nitrates.

name: Anti-Aging / Longevity / Healthspan
notes: Evidence-informed but largely off-label. Intermittent low-dose rapamycin (mTOR) has the strongest animal lifespan data; metformin for insulin sensitivity (TAME). NAD+/NMN for cellular energy. Set expectations: human longevity outcome data are limited.

name: Fatigue / Low Energy / Cellular Energy
workup: rule out anemia, hypothyroidism, hypogonadism, sleep apnea, depression
CBC, ferritin, TSH, testosterone, vitamin D, B12
notes: Always rule out treatable medical causes first. NAD+ (IV/SC) and NMN support mitochondrial energy. GH secretagogues can aid recovery and sleep quality where appropriate.

name: Injury Recovery / Joint & Tendon Healing
notes: Repair peptides (BPC-157 ± TB-500) are investigational/off-label with limited human data and current FDA compounding restrictions — document informed consent. Combine with rehab/physical therapy.

name: Growth Hormone Optimization / Body Composition / Sleep
notes: GH secretagogues preserve physiologic feedback vs exogenous rhGH. Keep IGF-1 in age-appropriate upper-normal range — not supraphysiologic. Tesamorelin specifically targets visceral fat.

name: Immune Support / Frequent Infections / Immunosenescence
notes: Thymosin alpha-1 supports T-cell function (investigational in US, approved abroad). Glutathione for antioxidant/detox support. Avoid immune-activating peptides in transplant recipients.

name: Chronic Pain / Inflammation / Autoimmune
avoidIf: current opioid therapy or opioid dependence (LDN precipitates withdrawal)
notes: LDN (1.5–4.5 mg nightly) modulates neuroinflammation; allow 6–8 weeks. Patient MUST be opioid-free. Emerging RCT support in fibromyalgia and Crohn's.

name: Hair Loss / Thinning
avoidIf: women who are or may become pregnant (finasteride teratogenicity)
significant cardiovascular disease / pericardial effusion (oral minoxidil)
workup: pattern/diagnosis (rule out telogen effluvium, thyroid, iron deficiency)
notes: Finasteride (lowers DHT) + low-dose oral minoxidil is a synergistic combination. Topical compounds (finasteride/minoxidil/GHK-Cu) reduce systemic exposure. Effects take 3–6 months.

name: Skin / Anti-Aging Dermatology
notes: Topical tretinoin is the gold-standard anti-aging active (collagen, turnover). GHK-Cu copper peptide for regeneration; glutathione for antioxidant/brightening. Daily SPF is mandatory with retinoids.
